title: Introduction to VaccineValidateR
text: VaccineValidateR package provides tools clean, standardize, validate childhood immunization data according US ACIP recommendations immunize.org guidance. supports: Mapping local immunization codes CVX codes Selecting filtering immunizations interest Evaluating dose validity, timing, series completion Generating population-level immunization metrics research quality improvement

title: Introduction to VaccineValidateR
text: can install development version GitHub: load package:

title: Introduction to VaccineValidateR
text: general workflow vaccine validation : Prepare cohort patients create_cohort(). Select immunizations using select_immunizations(). Validate immunizations ACIP rules using validation(). Evaluate visits identify missed delayed vaccines evaluate_visits(). Generate metrics reporting population_metrics().

title: Introduction to VaccineValidateR
code: library(data.table) library(VaccineValidateR)  # Example patient data patients <- data.table(   STUDY_ID = c(1, 2),   DOB = as.Date(c("2015-01-01", "2018-05-10")) )  # Example immunization data immunizations <- data.table(   STUDY_ID = c(1, 1, 2),   PRODUCT = c("MMR", "DTaP", "Polio"),   DATE_GIVEN = as.Date(c("2016-02-01", "2016-03-01", "2019-07-01")),   CVX = c(3, 20, 10) )  # Step 1: Create cohort cohort <- create_cohort(patients)  # Step 2: Select immunizations selected <- select_immunizations(   immunization_data = immunizations,   antigen_to_eval = c("MMR", "TETANUS", "POLIO") )  # Step 3: Validate validation_results <- validation(selected, patients = cohort)  # Step 4: Evaluate visits visit_results <- evaluate_visits(visit_data = immunizations, antigens = validation_results$antigens)  # Step 5: Metrics metrics <- population_metrics(patients = cohort, antigens = validation_results$antigens)  head(metrics)

title: Introduction to VaccineValidateR
text: VaccineValidateR enables transparent reproducible workflows immunization data validation, supporting research clinical quality improvement initiatives.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jeremy J Michel. Maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Michel JJ (2025). VaccineValidateR: Vaccine Validator United States Childhood Immunization Schedules. R package version 0.0.1.0, https://psyllix.github.io/VaccineValidateR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {VaccineValidateR: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules},   author = {Jeremy J Michel},   year = {2025},   note = {R package version 0.0.1.0},   url = {https://psyllix.github.io/VaccineValidateR}, }

what: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: VaccineValidateR R package testing vaccine histories ACIP immunization recommendations. designed research data quality purposes — clinical decision-making. Disclaimer: package official ACIP/CDC tool used clinical decision support. Use limited research, evaluation, educational contexts.

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: CVX codes updated 09/01/2025 Handles non-US immunization products without CVX codes Applies age interval rules recent valid ACIP(updated 09/01/2025) guidance antigen/dose. Considers edge cases situational handling per guidance Immunize.org Live vaccine minimum spacing across multiple series Meningitis B product-specific timing calculations Evaluates delayed delivery dose--dose (e.g. first dose delayed, calculation subsequent dose delays uses date first administration assess delay) Flags series completion main childhood antigens immunizations (product-level data mapping back content input) antigens (dose-level validated data) skipped_antigens (administrations meet ACIP timing - interval doses ignored doses given travel part non-US schedules) Used validation Identifies missed opportunities whether immunizations given visit delayed Notes future dose given wthin 15, 30, 90 days missed opportunity Considers combination visit antigen given visit Used validation* Computes composite completion measures (e.g., HEDIS child/adolescent metrics, UTD status evaluations, special popluation metrics). Aggregates visit-level results antigen (counts, % missed, % caught within X days), grouped year, antigen, custom grouping columns (e.g. SYSTEM/VISIT_TYPE). Designed support new ACIP changes, additional antigens, site/network-level QI. Scales large datasets (tested >60M administrations)

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: package CRAN. can install directly GitHub using: ```r # install.packages(“devtools”) devtools::install_github(“psyllix/VaccineValidateR”)

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: r library(VaccineValidateR)

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: data <- data.table::data.table( STUDY_ID = 1, CVX = c(“8”, “8”),#HEPATITIS B AGE_IMM_GIVEN = c(“0”,“31”), DATE_GIVEN = .Date(c(“2022-01-01”, “2022-02-01”))#BIRTH 1 MONTH DOSE )

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: result <- VaccineValidateR::validation(data)

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: head(result$antigens)

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: package updated response changes ACIP rules yearly (sometimes frequently). Bug/error fixes released notices. Validation rules based ACIP 2025 guidance, may yet cover every nuance. COVID vaccination status calculated completion given recent changes yearly formulations.

title: Vaccine Validator for United States Childhood Immunization Schedules
text: tables summarize minimum ages, intervals, series-completion rules common antigens. Detailed dose timing series completion tables provided dose reference vignette.

title: Antigen return schema — ANTIGEN_RETURN_DEFS
text: Constants defining expected structure antigen-level validation outputs, including column names human-readable definitions.

title: Antigen return schema — ANTIGEN_RETURN_DEFS
Antigen return schema — ANTIGEN_RETURN_DEFS
text: ANTIGEN_RETURN_COLUMNS Character vector required column names. ANTIGEN_RETURN_DEFS Named character vector mapping column         short description meaning. constants used internally validation, consistency checks, population-level metric calculations. constants used internally validation, consistency checks, population-level metric calculations.

title: Build CVX Mapping List — build_cvx_map
text: Utility function create structured list CVX code groups antigen interest. function called automatically package load populate global constant CVX.

title: Build CVX Mapping List — build_cvx_map
Build CVX Mapping List — build_cvx_map
text: lim_cvx_map Optional data.table containing local immunization identifiers (LIM) mapped CVX codes. Must include least PRODUCT CVX.

title: Build CVX Mapping List — build_cvx_map
text: named list CVX code vectors (cvx), corresponding antigen   grouped antigen set (e.g., HIB, POLIO, COVID,   LIVE_NON_ENTERAL).

title: Build CVX Mapping List — build_cvx_map
text: users need call directly. refresh global mapping (e.g., custom LIM→CVX file), use update_cvx_map instead. mapping combines reference CVX–antigen definitions bundled extdata/ optional local LIM→CVX mapping provided user. result assigned CVX package loads, making mapping available globally.

title: Build CVX Mapping List — build_cvx_map
code: # Normally not needed, CVX is available after library load: CVX$HIB #> Error: object 'CVX' not found  # Rebuild manually (not usually recommended) custom_map <- build_cvx_map() custom_map$POLIO #>  [1]    2   10   89  110  120  130  130  132  146  160  178  179  180  182  324 #> [16] 1002 1003  # Supported way to refresh the global mapping update_cvx_map() #> Error in assign("cvx", new_map, envir = parent.env(environment())): cannot change value of locked binding for 'cvx'

title: Validate Antigen Data Table — check_antigen_table
text: Internal utility confirm provided antigen-level data.table matches expected schema validation().

title: Validate Antigen Data Table — check_antigen_table
Validate Antigen Data Table — check_antigen_table
text: antigens data.table antigen-level immunization records, typically validation().

title: Validate Antigen Data Table — check_antigen_table
text: Invisibly returns TRUE validation passes.   Otherwise, throws error list missing columns.

title: Validate Antigen Data Table — check_antigen_table
text: Checks table data.table, contains VALID column, includes required fields ANTIGEN_RETURN_COLUMNS.

title: Validate Antigen Data Table — check_antigen_table

what: Generate Cohort List with Age Calculations — create_cohort
title: Generate Cohort List with Age Calculations — create_cohort
text: function generates cohort list dataset containing patient identifiers dates birth. validates required columns, optionally renames , ensures date consistency, calculates age days years.

title: Generate Cohort List with Age Calculations — create_cohort
code: create_cohort(   patients,   date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",   study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",   reference_date = Sys.Date(),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Generate Cohort List with Age Calculations — create_cohort
text: patients data.table containing least patient identifiers date birth. date_of_birth_column_name String. Name DOB column patients. Default "DOB". study_id_column_name String. Name patient identifier column. Default "STUDY_ID". reference_date Optional. Date used age calculation. Default Sys.Date(). verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Generate Cohort List with Age Calculations — create_cohort
text: data.table one row per patient, including: DOB: earliest date birth (Date) AGE_DAYS: age days reference_date AGE_YEARS: age years (decimal, using YEAR_LENGTH)

title: Generate Cohort List with Age Calculations — create_cohort
code: library(data.table)  # Example patient data dt <- data.table(   STUDY_ID = c(1, 2, 3),   DOB = c("2020-01-15", "2019/07/30", "15-Mar-2018") )  # Create cohort with default reference date (today) cohort <- create_cohort(dt) #> Starting patient cohort list development. Data validation step... #> Warning: Dropped 2 row(s) with missing/unparseable DOB. #> Data validated. Generating cohort. #> Cohort list development complete. N = 1 print(cohort) #>    STUDY_ID        DOB AGE_DAYS AGE_YEARS #>       <num>     <Date>    <int>     <num> #> 1:        1 2020-01-15     2071  5.670089  # Specify a fixed reference date for reproducibility cohort_fixed <- create_cohort(dt, reference_date = as.Date("2025-01-01")) #> Starting patient cohort list development. Data validation step... #> Warning: Dropped 2 row(s) with missing/unparseable DOB. #> Data validated. Generating cohort. #> Cohort list development complete. N = 1 print(cohort_fixed) #>    STUDY_ID        DOB AGE_DAYS AGE_YEARS #>       <num>     <Date>    <int>     <num> #> 1:        1 2020-01-15     1813  4.963723

title: CVX Mapping — cvx
text: named list CVX codes grouped antigen.

title: CVX Mapping — cvx
CVX Mapping — cvx
text: named list integer vectors. element corresponds   antigen grouped set antigens (e.g., HIB,   PCV, PPV23, LIVE_NON_ENTERAL).

title: CVX Mapping — cvx
text: object built automatically package load .onLoad() using build_cvx_map provides convenient access antigen-specific CVX code sets (e.g., CVX$HIB, CVX$POLIO). mapping combines reference CVX–antigen definitions bundled extdata/ optional local LIM→CVX mapping provided user build time.

title: CVX Mapping — cvx
code: # Access Hib CVX codes CVX$HIB #> Error: object 'CVX' not found  # Access all group names names(CVX) #> Error: object 'CVX' not found

what: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
text: function evaluates list visits determine whether immunization given given, , whether delayed, antigen level.

title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
code: evaluate_visits(   visit_data,   antigens,   patients = NULL,   full_return = FALSE,   antigen_to_eval = c("ALL"),   study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",   date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",   visit_date_column_name = "VISIT_DATE",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
text: visit_data data.table visit records. Must contain least study ID, DOB (linked via patients), visit date column. antigens data.table antigen-level results, typically antigens element validate_immunizations(). Required. Must include ANTIGEN, DATE_GIVEN, VALID, series/delay fields (see ANTIGEN_RETURN_COLUMNS). patients data.table patient info, least STUDY_ID DOB. Required DOB present visit_data. full_return Logical. Default FALSE. TRUE, returns visit–antigen combinations. FALSE, excludes rows patient aged , series completed previously, visit occurred earliest allowable dose date. antigen_to_eval Character vector antigens evaluate. Default "". study_id_column_name Name column visit_data containing study IDs. Default "STUDY_ID". date_of_birth_column_name Name column visit_data dates birth. Default "DOB". visit_date_column_name Name column visit_data visit dates. Default "VISIT_DATE". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints messages processing. Default TRUE.

title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
text: data.table containing: standardized columns VISIT_RETURN_COLUMNS original columns visit_data Temporary evaluation fields excluded.

title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
text: full_return = FALSE, output excludes visits : patient aged antigen series already completed visit visit occurred minimum allowable dose date keeps visit–antigen combinations meaningfully evaluated. returned data.table always contains standardized set columns defined VISIT_RETURN_COLUMNS, plus columns originally present visit_data. Temporary internal columns (e.g., JOIN_DATE, TOMORROW, YESTERDAY) dropped.

title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
text: Using full_return = TRUE can produce large outputs run slowly, since evaluates every possible visit–antigen pair regardless clinical relevance. Use caution large cohorts.

title: Evaluate Visits for Immunization Status — evaluate_visits
code: library(data.table)  # Minimal toy data visits <- data.table(   STUDY_ID = 1,   VISIT_DATE = as.Date("2025-01-01"),   DOB = as.Date("2024-01-01") ) # antigens is an output from \code{\link{validate_immunizations}}  # Evaluate visits (full return = FALSE trims unnecessary rows) evaluate_visits(visits, antigens, patients = NULL, full_return = FALSE) #> Starting evaluation process for visits. Validating inputs. #> validate_immunizations check complete - formatting data. This may take a while... #>  Processed 7 groups out of 15. 47% done. Time elapsed: 3s. ETA: 4s. title: Invalid Dose Columns — INVALID_DOSE_COLUMNS
text: Standardized column set invalid dose records. Used consistently across functions identify, store, report invalid excluded immunization doses.

title: Invalid Dose Columns — INVALID_DOSE_COLUMNS
Invalid Dose Columns — INVALID_DOSE_COLUMNS
text: character vector column names: STUDY_ID Unique patient identifier PRODUCT Immunization product name CVX CVX vaccine code ANTIGEN Standardized antigen grouping TABLE_INDEX Row index original processing table GIVEN_STATUS 1 given, 0 ADMIN_LOCATION Location vaccine administered DATE_GIVEN Date immunization given AGE_IMM_GIVEN Age days immunization ABS_ADMIN_COUNTER Absolute order doses patient INTERVAL Interval prior dose (days) LIVE_INTERVAL Interval prior live dose (days) COUNTER Internal counter validation cycles CYCLE Cycle ID dose processed multiple iterations SERIES_COMPLETE TRUE antigen series marked complete NOTES Free-text notes dose flagged invalid

title: Evaluate Population-Level Immunization Metrics — population_metrics
text: function evaluates childhood adolescent immunization coverage HEDIS Non-HEDIS metrics (UTD Interval/Dose Calculations) calculates whether patient meets specific vaccine coverage criteria (e.g., HPV, Tetanus, MCV, DTaP, PCV, Rota, Influenza) HEDIS Non-Hedis (direct calculation) standards based provided antigen data age 13 (HEDIS ADOL) age 2 (HEDIS CIS).

title: Evaluate Population-Level Immunization Metrics — population_metrics
code: population_metrics(   patients,   antigens,   date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",   study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Evaluate Population-Level Immunization Metrics — population_metrics
text: patients data.table patients least study ID column. antigens data.table validated antigen-level immunization records (output validate_immunizations functions) including AGE_IMM_GIVEN, DOSE_COUNTER, DOSE_COMPLETES_SERIES. date_of_birth_column_name Name column patients containing patient DOB. Default 'DOB'. study_id_column_name Name column patients containing study ID. Default 'STUDY_ID'. verbose Logical indicating whether print log messages. Default TRUE.

title: Evaluate Population-Level Immunization Metrics — population_metrics
text: patients data.table additional columns HEDIS UTD metric,   including adolescent young child measures (e.g., HEDIS_ADOL2, UTD_ADOL2, HEDIS_CIS10, UTD_CIS10).

title: Vaccine schedule reference dates — RSV_DATE
text: Important implementation guidance change dates used dose validation.

title: Vaccine schedule reference dates — RSV_DATE
Vaccine schedule reference dates — RSV_DATE
text: Constants class Date. RSV_DATE 2023-09-01, Beyfortus release (prior = palivizumab). MENB_DATE 2024-10-24, MenB guidance change. OPV_DATE 2016-04-01, OPV rule update. HPV_DATE 2016-04-01, HPV schedule update. PCV_DATE 2010-02-24, PCV13 release.

what: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
title: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
text: Filters processes immunization data mapping antigens CVX codes, ensuring correct immunization data processed returned.

title: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
code: select_immunizations(   immunization_data,   lim_cvx_map = NULL,   antigen_to_eval = c("ALL"),   verbose = TRUE,   date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",   study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",   immunization_code_column_name = "CVX",   local_immunization_identifier_column_name = "LIM",   immunization_product_name_column_name = "PRODUCT",   immunization_date_given_column_name = "DATE_GIVEN",   age_at_immunization_column_name = "AGE_IMM_GIVEN" )

title: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
text: immunization_data data.table immunizations containing minimum STUDY_ID, PRODUCT, either DATE_GIVEN AGE_IMM_GIVEN. lim_cvx_map Optional data.table local-code→CVX mappings. Default NULL. Used 'CVX' present immunization data. antigen_to_eval Character vector antigens evaluate (e.g., c('POLIO','MMR')) '' (case-insensitive). verbose Logical indicating whether print log messages. Default TRUE. date_of_birth_column_name Character string specifying column containing date birth. Default 'DOB'. study_id_column_name Character string specifying column containing study ID. Default 'STUDY_ID'. immunization_code_column_name Character string specifying column containing CVX codes. Default 'CVX'. local_immunization_identifier_column_name Character string specifying column containing local immunization identifiers. Default 'LIM'. immunization_product_name_column_name Character string specifying column containing immunization product names. Default 'PRODUCT'. immunization_date_given_column_name Character string specifying column containing date immunization given. Default 'DATE_GIVEN'. age_at_immunization_column_name Character string specifying column containing age immunization. Default 'AGE_IMM_GIVEN'.

title: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
text: data.table containing refined filtered immunization data added attribute processed indicating table ready.   attribute antigens_used (character vector) attached, recording   antigens evaluated.

title: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
text: Antigen selection special handling live, non-enteral vaccines (e.g., MMR, VZV, intranasal INFLUENZA). included antigen_to_eval present dataset, live antigens retained together. ensures live-virus scheduling rules (e.g., spacing doses) preserved downstream validate_immunizations.

title: Filter and Process Immunization Data — select_immunizations
code: library(data.table)  # Example immunization data imm_dt <- data.table(   STUDY_ID   = c(1, 2, 3, 3),   DOB        = as.Date(c("2020-01-15", "2019-07-30", "2018-03-15", "2018-03-15")),   PRODUCT    = c("MMR Vax", "DTaP", "Polio", "Varicella"),   CVX        = c(03, 20, 10, 21),           # simplified CVX codes   DATE_GIVEN = as.Date(c("2021-02-01", "2020-09-01", "2020-05-01", "2021-06-01")) )  # Select all immunizations selected_all <- select_immunizations(imm_dt) #> CVX codes employed to determine antigens within immunizations. Merging data. Please Wait... #> Content restricted to antigens of interest. Finalizing content. #> This table can be sent to validate_immunizations function directly and will not be reprocessed. #> Do not change 'processed' attribute. N=4 print(selected_all) #>    STUDY_ID        DOB   PRODUCT   CVX DATE_GIVEN  POLIO    HIB    PCV   HEPA #>       <num>     <Date>    <char> <num>     <Date> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> #> 1:        1 2020-01-15   MMR Vax     3 2021-02-01  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE #> 2:        2 2019-07-30      DTaP    20 2020-09-01  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE #> 3:        3 2018-03-15     Polio    10 2020-05-01   TRUE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE #> 4:        3 2018-03-15 Varicella    21 2021-06-01  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE #>      HEPB    HPV    MCV   MENB TETANUS   ROTA  COVID    RSV    MMR    VZV #>    <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl>  <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> #> 1:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   TRUE  FALSE #> 2:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE    TRUE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE #> 3:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE #> 4:  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE  FALSE   TRUE #>    INFLUENZA GIVEN_STATUS ADMIN_LOCATION #>       <lgcl>        <num>         <lgcl> #> 1:     FALSE            1             NA #> 2:     FALSE            1             NA #> 3:     FALSE            1             NA #> 4:     FALSE            1             NA  # Select only POLIO and MMR antigens selected_subset <- select_immunizations(imm_dt, antigen_to_eval = c("POLIO", "MMR"))#will also return Varicella(VZV) since any live nonenteral antigen forces all to be kept #> CVX codes employed to determine antigens within immunizations. Merging data. Please Wait... #> Content restricted to antigens of interest. Finalizing content. #> This table can be sent to validate_immunizations function directly and will not be reprocessed. #> Do not change 'processed' attribute. N=3 print(selected_subset) #>    STUDY_ID        DOB   PRODUCT   CVX DATE_GIVEN  POLIO    MMR    VZV #>       <num>     <Date>    <char> <num>     <Date> <lgcl> <lgcl> <lgcl> #> 1:        1 2020-01-15   MMR Vax     3 2021-02-01  FALSE   TRUE  FALSE #> 2:        3 2018-03-15     Polio    10 2020-05-01   TRUE  FALSE  FALSE #> 3:        3 2018-03-15 Varicella    21 2021-06-01  FALSE  FALSE   TRUE #>    INFLUENZA GIVEN_STATUS ADMIN_LOCATION #>       <lgcl>        <num>         <lgcl> #> 1:     FALSE            1             NA #> 2:     FALSE            1             NA #> 3:     FALSE            1             NA

title: Summarize Population-Level Immunization Metrics — summarize_population_metrics
text: function summarizes patient-level population immunization metrics (created population_metrics()) across evaluation years, optionally returning results long wide format. Metrics grouped evaluation year (e.g., age 2 CIS, age 13 ADOL).

title: Summarize Population-Level Immunization Metrics — summarize_population_metrics
code: summarize_population_metrics(   patients,   visits,   years,   metrics_of_interest = NULL,   id_col = "STUDY_ID",   dob_col = "DOB",   verbose = TRUE,   output_format = c("long", "wide") )

title: Summarize Population-Level Immunization Metrics — summarize_population_metrics
text: patients data.table patient-level records already processed population_metrics() (contains metric columns attached attribute population_metric_definitions). visits data.table patient visits including least STUDY_ID VISIT_DATE. years numeric vector evaluation years include summary. metrics_of_interest Character vector metric names (e.g., "HEDIS_ADOL2", "UTD_CIS10"). must exist columns patients. specified, return possible metrics patient table. id_col Column name patients identifying unique patients. Default "STUDY_ID". dob_col Column name patients containing date birth. Default "DOB". verbose Logical; TRUE, progress messages printed. Default TRUE. output_format Either "long" (default; stacked rows metric labels) "wide" (one row per year, metrics columns).

title: Summarize Population-Level Immunization Metrics — summarize_population_metrics
text: data.table summarizing requested metrics: "long" format: one row per metric-year counts, percents,       metric labels. "wide" format: one row per evaluation year, columns       metric.

title: Summarize Population-Level Immunization Metrics — summarize_population_metrics
text: Evaluation age inferred metric name: CIS metrics ("CIS") → evaluated age 2 ADOL metrics ("ADOL") → evaluated age 13 metrics → default age 18

title: Summarize Visit-Level Immunization Evaluations — summarize_visit_evaluations
text: Summarize output evaluate_visits(), producing counts percentages visits vaccines due, given, missed. Grouping can flexibly specified flags additional grouping columns.

title: Summarize Visit-Level Immunization Evaluations — summarize_visit_evaluations
code: summarize_visit_evaluations(   visit_output,   antigens_of_interest = NULL,   by_year = TRUE,   by_antigen = TRUE,   group_cols = NULL,   percent_var = "PCT_GIVEN_IF_DUE",   verbose = TRUE,   output_format = c("long", "wide") )

title: Summarize Visit-Level Immunization Evaluations — summarize_visit_evaluations
text: visit_output data.table returned evaluate_visits(). Must contain standardized columns defined VISIT_RETURN_COLUMNS, including ANTIGEN, VISIT_DATE, DUE, GIVEN, MISSED. Must also attribute "processed" set. antigens_of_interest Character vector antigen names include. Default NULL, case antigens data used. by_year Logical; TRUE, group VISIT_YEAR. Default TRUE. by_antigen Logical; TRUE, group ANTIGEN. Default TRUE. group_cols Optional character vector additional grouping column names. percent_var Character; percentage column pivot wide format. Default "PCT_GIVEN_IF_DUE". valid options "PCT_MISSED_IF_DUE", "PCT_WITHIN_15_DAYS_IF_MISSED", "PCT_WITHIN_30_DAYS_IF_MISSED", "PCT_WITHIN_90_DAYS_IF_MISSED". verbose Logical; TRUE, progress messages printed. Default TRUE. output_format Either "long" (default; stacked rows antigen labels) "wide" (one row per group, antigens columns).

title: Summarize Visit-Level Immunization Evaluations — summarize_visit_evaluations
text: data.table summarizing requested antigens: "long" format: one row per group counts, percents, labels. "wide" format: one row per grouping set, columns antigen.

title: Summarize Visit-Level Immunization Evaluations — summarize_visit_evaluations

title: Update the CVX Mapping — update_cvx_map
text: Rebuilds global CVX constant optional local LIM→CVX map. updates copy inside package namespace downstream functions see new mapping.

title: Update the CVX Mapping — update_cvx_map
Update the CVX Mapping — update_cvx_map
text: lim_cvx_map Optional data.table containing local immunization identifiers (LIM) mapped CVX codes. Must include least PRODUCT CVX.

title: Update the CVX Mapping — update_cvx_map
text: Invisibly returns updated CVX object.

title: Update the CVX Mapping — update_cvx_map
code: # Refresh CVX mapping with default references update_cvx_map() #> Error in assign("cvx", new_map, envir = parent.env(environment())): cannot change value of locked binding for 'cvx'  # Refresh with custom local LIM→CVX mapping my_map <- data.table::data.table(LIM = "X123", PRODUCT = "Test", CVX = 999) update_cvx_map(my_map) #> Error in assign("cvx", new_map, envir = parent.env(environment())): cannot change value of locked binding for 'cvx'  # Verify it updated cvx$CUSTOM <- 999

what: Evaluate Immunizations Against ACIP Schedule — validate_immunizations
title: Evaluate Immunizations Against ACIP Schedule — validate_immunizations
text: function takes list immunizations evaluates minimum intervals based ACIP schedule, determining immunity status across list antigens. live virus products included, live-virus-containing products evaluated. Either patient list dates birth within patient list required. patient list returned immunization data. Either date immunization given age immunization days required. Using age days notably faster large datasets. Either CVX codes map local immunization antigen data CVX codes required. Default column names can overwritten using individual parameters. Please use column names across files!

title: Evaluate Immunizations Against ACIP Schedule — validate_immunizations
code: validate_immunizations(   immunization_data,   patients,   lim_cvx_map,   antigen_to_eval = c("ALL"),   verbose = TRUE,   date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",   study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",   immunization_code_column_name = "CVX",   local_immunization_identifier_column_name = "LIM",   immunization_product_name_column_name = "PRODUCT",   immunization_date_given_column_name = "DATE_GIVEN",   age_at_immunization_column_name = "AGE_IMM_GIVEN",   reference_date = Sys.Date() )

title: Evaluate Immunizations Against ACIP Schedule — validate_immunizations
text: immunization_data data.table immunizations containing minimum STUDY_ID, PRODUCT, either DATE_GIVEN AGE_IMM_GIVEN. patient table, DOB column required. CVX, GIVEN_STATUS, ADMIN_LOCATION columns optional. patients Optional data.table patient info, DOB. Default NULL, case DOB must present visit_data. lim_cvx_map Optional data.table mapping local immunization identifiers (LIM) CVX codes. Required CVX present immunization data. Must include PRODUCT CVX. Default NULL, case system assume immunization data valid CVX' verbose Logical. Default TRUE. TRUE, console messages printed step. immunization_code_column_name Column name containing CVX codes. Default "CVX". local_immunization_identifier_column_name Column name containing local immunization identifiers. Default "LIM". immunization_product_name_column_name Column name containing product names. Default "PRODUCT". immunization_date_given_column_name Column name containing date immunization given. Default "DATE_GIVEN". age_at_immunization_column_name Column name containing age immunization days. Default "AGE_IMM_GIVEN". reference_date Date cutoff immunizations. Default Sys.Date(). antigens_to_eval Character vector antigens evaluate. Default "".

title: Evaluate Immunizations Against ACIP Schedule — validate_immunizations
text: named list three data.table elements: immunizations Product-level immunization data processing.     Typical columns include: STUDY_ID – patient identifier PRODUCT – vaccine product name CVX – vaccine CVX code (integer) DATE_GIVEN – immunization administration date AGE_IMM_GIVEN – age days administration Antigen flags (e.g., POLIO, MMR, HIB) indicating antigen mapping antigens Antigen-level dose data including validation checks.     Typical columns include: STUDY_ID, ANTIGEN, DOB, PRODUCT, CVX DATE_GIVEN, AGE_IMM_GIVEN DOSE_COUNTER, ABS_ADMIN_COUNTER, TABLE_INDEX Validation fields: VALID, SERIES_COMPLETE, DELAYED, NOTES Interval tracking: INTERVAL, LIVE_INTERVAL, CYCLE, NEXT_DOSE_MIN, NEXT_DOSE_RECOMMENDED skipped_antigens Immunizations excluded evaluation due failing rules     (e.g., given birth, given early, live vaccine spacing violation).     Contains structure antigens plus NOTES indicating reason.
